meta.title: Civic Tips
meta.description: Small habits, massive impact. Here's what you CAN and CANNOT do as a responsible citizen.

items.title: Carry a Trash Bag
items.text: Keep a small bag in your car or bag to collect wrappers, bottles and waste until you find a bin. Zero excuses to litter.

items.title: Report Violations Online
items.text: Use apps like Swachhata App, FixMyStreet or your municipality's portal to report potholes, illegal dumping and broken streetlights.

items.title: Stop at Red Lights — Always
items.text: Even at 2 AM. Even when no one is watching. Traffic rules save lives — yours and others'. Plus, cameras are everywhere now.

items.title: Fix Leaking Taps
items.text: A single leaking tap wastes 2,000+ litres a month. Water is a national resource. Report water leaks in public spaces immediately.

items.title: Don't Honk Unnecessarily
items.text: Honking in traffic jams doesn't make the jam disappear — it just increases collective stress, anger, and noise pollution for everyone.

items.title: Don't Block Footpaths
items.text: Parking cars on footpaths forces pedestrians — including elderly and disabled people — onto dangerous roads. It's illegal and inconsiderate.

items.title: Vote in Every Election
items.text: From Panchayat to Parliament, your vote shapes the infrastructure, services and civic environment around you. Skipping is not an option.

items.title: Segregate Your Waste
items.text: Wet waste, dry waste and hazardous waste go into separate bins. It takes 10 seconds and makes recycling possible. Mixing is lazy AND harmful.

items.title: Use Zebra Crossings
items.text: Cross the road only at zebra crossings or designated pedestrian crossings whenever possible. It keeps you safe, improves traffic flow, and encourages drivers to respect pedestrian rights.
